evidence.text: Labs: lactate 2.2 mmol/L, creatinine 1.2 mg/dL, platelets 120 10^9/L. Patient is being treated with antibiotics for a suspected pneumonia. Chest X-ray shows bilateral infiltrates. Patient is being closely monitored for signs of sepsis.

Patient was diagnosed with sepsis due to pneumonia. Patient was treated with antibiotics and vasopressors. Patient's condition improved with treatment. Labs: lactate 1.5 mmol/L, creatinine 1.0 mg/dL, platelets 150 10^9/L.

Patient's condition has not improved with antibiotics. Labs: lactate 2.5 mmol/L, creatinine 1.5 mg/dL, platelets 100 10^9/L. Vital signs: temperature 39 C, MAP 60 mmHg, HR 120 bpm. Patient is being started on vasopressors to support blood pressure.

Patient's family was educated on signs of sepsis and when to seek medical attention. Nursing: patient tolerating oral intake as ordered. Nursing: skin warm and dry; no new rashes documented. Nursing: pain score reviewed; analgesia as needed.

## Coding rationale
Clinical validation of billed Sepsis should rest on documented infection plus organ dysfunction indicators (sepsis3_summary, Section: Sepsis coding notes (high level)). Findings that affect payment grouping must be supported by chart indicators (sepsis3_summary, Section: Organ dysfunction indicators (simplified for demo)). This draft is for auditor review only and is not a payment decision. Key evidence IDs considered: E1, E10, E11, E12, E13, E14, E15, E16, E17, E18, E19, E2, E3, E4, E5, E6, E7, E8, E9.
